summary: Retry the bounded strong-pi pulsed ODMR alignment screen on the freshly re-imaged and freshly tracked r01 candidate.
scientific_rationale: The prior pODMR did not acquire data because the target count gate collapsed. Fresh Imaging recovered the same/local r01 candidate, and standalone TrackCenter just succeeded at 38.971 kcps at [114.531351,117.705596,116.283685] um. The human requested magnetic-field alignment judged by strong-pi pODMR. The quantitative planning calculation remains applicable for a screen around the expected ms=+1 resonance near 3.875 GHz: expected B about 358.6 G, 13C Larmor about 0.384 MHz, and strong-pi time about 50 ns, so length_rabi_pulse=52 ns with mod_depth=1 should make a clear resonance-scale feature visible if this candidate is aligned.
comparison_targets: prior pODMR count-gate failure evidence, now addressed by fresh re-image and TrackCenter
expected_evidence: terminal savedexperiment and raw readout export for strong-pi pODMR
raw/readout-aware evidence for or against a clear resonance in 3.825-3.925 GHz
success_criteria: bridge execute completes with nonzero acquired data
raw readouts show a clear resonance-scale feature consistent with expected strong-pi contrast and above noise/baseline variation
no tracking/count-collapse/hardware anomaly invalidates the data
stop_conditions: bridge queue becomes occupied before submission
verifier or MATLAB advisory blocks execute
RunExperiment aborts or returns zero averages/no saved data
bridge_action: execute managed single-item batch for pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1
